content.problemStatement:
Akash loves going to school, but not on weekends.

A week consists of 777 days (Monday to Sunday). Akash takes a leave every **Saturday**.

If a month consists of NNN days and the **first-day** of the month is **Monday**, find the number of days Akash would take a leave in the whole month.


Input Format

First line will contain TTT, the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
Each test case contains a single line of input, one integer NNN - the number of days in the month.


Output Format
For each test case, output in a single line, the number of days Akash would take a leave in the whole month.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer N, the number of days in the month. The first day of the month is always Monday.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the number of Saturdays (leaves) in that month.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For N=5 there is no Saturday. For N=6 the 6th day is Saturday → 1 leave. For N=8 the first Saturday is day 6, the next day is Monday, so still only 1 leave. For N=22 there are 3 Saturdays (days 6,13,20).
N=1 → 0 Saturdays. N=7 includes exactly one Saturday (day 6). N=13 has one full week (1 Saturday) plus 6 extra days, giving a second Saturday on day 13.
For 1,000,000,000 days: 1,000,000,000/7 = 142,857,142 weeks, remainder 6 → one extra Saturday, total 142,857,143. For 999,999,999 days: remainder 5 → no extra, total 142,857,142.

solution.approach: Since the month always starts on Monday, the days repeat every 7 days. In each full week there is exactly one Saturday. Let fullWeeks = N / 7 (integer division) and extraDays = N % 7. The answer is fullWeeks plus one more if extraDays >= 6 (because the 6th day of a week is Saturday). Formula: answer = N / 7 + (N % 7 >= 6 ? 1 : 0). This runs in O(1) per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work for each test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are used.

code.Python3:
import sys

def main():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    t = int(data[0])
    out = []
    for i in range(1, t+1):
        n = int(data[i])
        ans = n//7 + (1 if n%7 >= 6 else 0)
        out.append(str(ans))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
